sections.title: STANDARD FOR PINEAPPLES
sections.text: Adopted in 1993. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR PINEAPPLES
level=1
Adopted in 1993. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.

page_spans.text: Adopted in 1993. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of pineapples grown from Ananas comosus (L.) Merr., of the Bromeliaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Pineapples for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of pineapples grown from Ananas comosus (L.) Merr., of the Bromeliaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Pineapples for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of pineapples grown from Ananas comosus (L.) Merr., of the Bromeliaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Pineapples for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The pineapples have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and/or commercial type and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The pineapples have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and/or commercial type and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the pineapples must be such as to enable them:  
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: Maturity Requirements
sections.text: The total soluble solids content in the fruit  flesh should be at least 12°Brix (twelve Brix degrees).  For the determination of Brix degrees a representative sample of the juice of all the fruit shall be taken.

level=3

page_spans.text: The total soluble solids content in the fruit  flesh should be at least 12°Brix (twelve Brix degrees).  For the determination of Brix degrees a representative sample of the juice of all the fruit shall be taken.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Pineapples in this class must be of superior quality.  They must be characteristic of the  variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the average weight of the fruit with a minimum weight of 700  g, except for small size varieties3, which can have a minimum weight of 250 g, in accordance with the following table: 
 
Size Code 
Average Weight (+/-12%) 
(in grams) 
with crown without crown

B 2300 1910 
C 1900 1580 
D 1600 1330 
E 1400 1160 
F 1200 1000 
G 1000  830 
H  800  660

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=2
Pineapples in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:  
- slight defects in shape; 
- slight defects in colouring, including sun spots; 
- slight skin defects (i.e., scratches, scars, scrapes and blemishes) not exceeding 4% of the total surface area. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit. 
The crown, if present, shall be simple and straight or slightly curved with no sprouts, and shall be between 50 and 150% of the length of the fruit for pineapples with trimmed or untrimmed3 crowns.

sections.title: Class II
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class II
number=2.2.3
level=2
This class includes pineapples which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above.  The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the pineapples retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: 
- defects in shape; 
- defects in colouring, including sun spots; 
- skin defects (i.e., scratches, scars, scrapes, bruises and blemishes), not exceeding 8% of the total surface area. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit. 
The crown, if present, shall be simple or double and straight or slightly curved, with no sprouts.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each inspection lot for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of pineapples corresponding to the size immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only pineapples of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform.  The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Pineapples must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly.  The materials used inside the package must be new4, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Pineapples shall be packed i n each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to  ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the pineapples. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).  7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).  7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for  Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.function: Requirement with Conditional Exception
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PINEAPPLES
pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento SEM seções internas. Texto sobre datas de adoção e revisão associado ao título principal.

Página conta com dois níveis principais de hierarquia: nível 1 com seções numeradas e níveis 2 e 3 com subseções claras, todas seguindo a numeração e títulos explícitos. Texto interno está associado aos respectivos títulos conforme esperado. A seção 2.2.1 continua para próxima página.

Página apresenta três seções principais mapeadas: 2.2.2 Class I, 2.2.3 Class II (subseções do 2.2) e 3 PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING (nível 1). Todos os textos são associados de forma clara e títulos numerados guiados pela numeração hierárquica. O cabeçalho repetitivo da página foi identificado e excluído do texto interno. Não há indicações de continuidade com a página anterior ou próxima nesta página.

A página apresenta uma estrutura clara com três seções principais numeradas 4, 5 e 6, cada uma com subseções e subsubseções visíveis e numeradas hierarquicamente. O conteúdo de texto está adequadamente associado a cada nó, preservando a ordem do documento e sem incluir o cabeçalho identificado. A seção 6 indica continuidade para a próxima página, marcada apropriadamente. Não há indicação de continuidade da página anterior nesta página.

A página 5 apresenta uma estrutura clara com as seções 6 (LABELING), 7 (CONTAMINANTS) e 8 (HYGIENE). A seção 6 possui subtítulos e subsubtítulos numerados conforme a hierarquia, associados corretamente com seus textos correspondentes. A seção 6 e parte de seus subtítulos indicam continuidade para a próxima página. As seções 7 e 8 aparecem completas nesta página. O cabeçalho 'CXS 182-1993   5' foi identificado e removido do texto interno. O título principal do documento não foi identificado na página.
